message:
style(tui): reformat responsive layout builders and use saturating_sub in screens

Apply rustfmt-consistent formatting to ResponsiveLayout builder chains
across all TUI screens (agents, analytics, archive_browser, attachments,
contacts, explorer, projects, reservations, system_health, threads,
tool_metrics). The previous formatting used inconsistent multi-line
patterns that diverged from the project's rustfmt configuration.

Replace bare subtraction with saturating_sub for shimmer animation age
calculations in dashboard, messages, and timeline screens, and for
thread velocity duration in threads screen, preventing underflow on
clock skew.

Reformat multi-line tuple arguments in projects, reservations, contacts,
system_health, and tool_metrics detail panels for consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

         let area = inner;
 
         // Responsive layout: single-col on narrow, table+detail on wide
-        let layout = ResponsiveLayout::new(
-            Flex::vertical().constraints([Constraint::Fill]),
-        )
-        .at(
-            Breakpoint::Lg,
-            Flex::horizontal().constraints([
-                Constraint::Percentage(60.0),
-                Constraint::Fill,
-            ]),
-        )
-        .at(
-            Breakpoint::Xl,
-            Flex::horizontal().constraints([
-                Constraint::Percentage(50.0),
-                Constraint::Fill,
-            ]),
-        );

+            .at(
+                Breakpoint::Lg,
+                Flex::horizontal().constraints([Constraint::Percentage(60.0), Constraint::Fill]),
+            )
+            .at(
+                Breakpoint::Xl,
+                Flex::horizontal().constraints([Constraint::Percentage(50.0), Constraint::Fill]),
+            );
 
         let split = layout.split(area);
         let table_area = split.rects[0];

@@ -956,30 +956,22 @@ impl MailScreen for ArchiveBrowserScreen {
         // Responsive tree/preview split using ResponsiveLayout breakpoints.
         // Xs–Sm (< 90): tree only. Md (90–119): 45/55. Lg (120–159): 40/60. Xl (160+): 35/65.
         let layout = if self.detail_visible {
-            ResponsiveLayout::new(
-                Flex::vertical().constraints([Constraint::Fill]),
-            )
-            .at(
-                Breakpoint::Md,
-                Flex::horizontal().constraints([
-                    Constraint::Percentage(45.0),
-                    Constraint::Percentage(55.0),
-                ]),
-            )
-            .at(
-                Breakpoint::Lg,
-                Flex::horizontal().constraints([
-                    Constraint::Percentage(40.0),
-                    Constraint::Percentage(60.0),
-                ]),
-            )
-            .at(
-                Breakpoint::Xl,
-                Flex::horizontal().constraints([
-                    Constraint::Percentage(35.0),
-                    Constraint::Percentage(65.0),
-                ]),
-            )
+            ResponsiveLayout::new(Flex::vertical().constraints([Constraint::Fill]))
+                .at(
+                    Breakpoint::Md,
+                    Flex::horizontal()
+                        .constraints([Constraint::Percentage(45.0), Constraint::Percentage(55.0)]),
+                )
+                .at(
+                    Breakpoint::Lg,
+                    Flex::horizontal()
+                        .constraints([Constraint::Percentage(40.0), Constraint::Percentage(60.0)]),
+                )
+                .at(
+                    Breakpoint::Xl,
+                    Flex::horizontal()
+                        .constraints([Constraint::Percentage(35.0), Constraint::Percentage(65.0)]),
+                )
         } else {
             ResponsiveLayout::new(Flex::vertical().constraints([Constraint::Fill]))
         };

             // Precision loss acceptable: microsecond timestamps and message counts
